skill.name: Business Case Generator
skill.description: Generate comprehensive investor-ready business case documents with market analysis, financial projections, and strategic planning
skill.summary: Creates investor-ready business cases covering market opportunity, solution, competitive analysis, financials, team, risks, and funding requirements
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis detected no suspicious patterns (risk score: 0/100). The skill declares standard tool access (Read, Write, Bash, WebSearch, WebFetch) for legitimate business document creation. No prompt injection attempts or malicious patterns found. Safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Startups need comprehensive business cases to secure funding and guide strategy, but creating investor-ready documents is time-consuming and complex. This skill streamlines business case creation with structured frameworks for market analysis, financial projections, and strategic planning.
actual_capabilities: Creates comprehensive 10-section business case documents with executive summary, market analysis, and financial projections
Generates investor-ready funding requests with use of proceeds breakdown and milestone timelines
Produces competitive analysis matrices and differentiation positioning
Develops financial models with unit economics, CAC, LTV, and 3-year projections
Structures team composition analysis with hiring plans and equity considerations
Identifies and documents risk factors with mitigation strategies
limitations: Requires user-provided data for accurate market sizing and financial projections
Cannot independently verify external market data or competitor information
Does not replace professional financial or legal advice for fundraising
Output quality depends on accuracy and completeness of user inputs

use_cases.title: Seed-stage startup fundraising
use_cases.description: Pre-seed and seed startups create investor-ready business cases for angel investors and early-stage VCs, including TAM/SAM/SOM analysis and 3-year financial projections.
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders preparing for seed funding rounds

use_cases.title: Strategic planning for product launches
use_cases.description: Product teams develop comprehensive business cases for new product initiatives, including competitive analysis, go-to-market strategy, and resource requirements.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and business development teams

use_cases.title: Internal project approval documentation
use_cases.description: Corporate teams create structured business cases for internal stakeholder approval, including ROI analysis, risk assessment, and implementation timelines.
use_cases.target_user: Business analysts and project managers in enterprises

prompt_templates.title: Basic business case overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a business case for [company name] that solves [problem] for [target customers]. Include executive summary, problem statement, solution overview, and basic market opportunity. We are at [stage] and seeking [funding amount].

prompt_templates.title: Competitive analysis and positioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a competitive analysis section for our business case. Our main competitors are [competitor list]. Our key differentiators are [differentiators]. Create a competitive matrix and positioning map showing how we compare on [factors].

prompt_templates.title: Financial projections with unit economics
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a 3-year financial projection model for a [business model type] business. Current metrics: [current MRR, customers, growth rate]. Industry benchmarks: [CAC, LTV, gross margin]. Include revenue model, expense projections, unit economics, and path to profitability.

prompt_templates.title: Complete investor-ready business case
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive investor-ready business case for [company]. Audience: [VCs/angels/strategic partners]. Stage: [pre-seed/seed/Series A]. Available materials: [pitch deck, financial model, market research]. Include all 10 sections: executive summary, problem and market opportunity, solution and product, competitive analysis, business model and GTM, financial projections, team and organization, traction and milestones, risks and mitigation, and funding request with use of proceeds.

output_examples.input: Create a business case for AcmeCorp, an AI-powered email marketing platform for e-commerce. We are seed-stage seeking $2M. Target customers are online retailers with $1M-10M annual revenue.
output: Executive Summary: AcmeCorp delivers AI-driven email marketing automation that increases e-commerce revenue by 35% through personalized campaign optimization.
Market Opportunity: TAM $4.2B (global email marketing), SAM $850M (e-commerce segment), SOM $42M (5% of SAM by Year 3)
Funding Ask: $2M seed round for product development (40%), sales and marketing (40%), operations (10%), working capital (10%). 18-month runway to $1.5M ARR milestone.

output_examples.input: Generate financial projections for a SaaS business with current $15K MRR, 45 customers, 12% monthly growth. Target: $500K ARR by Year 2.
output: Year 2 Revenue: $520K ARR (35 customers/month, $1.2K ACV, 85% retention)
Year 3 Revenue: $1.4M ARR (60 customers/month, $1.5K ACV, 90% retention)
Unit Economics: CAC $2,400, LTV $14,500, LTV:CAC 6.0x, CAC Payback 5 months, Gross Margin 82%

best_practices: Lead with customer problem and quantify pain with specific data points before presenting your solution
Support all market size claims with cited sources and clearly state calculation methodology
Acknowledge risks and competition honestly with concrete mitigation strategies rather than dismissing concerns
anti_patterns: Using unsupported claims or generic statements without data backing (for example: huge market without TAM calculation)
Ignoring or downplaying competition instead of clearly articulating differentiation and defensibility
Creating overly optimistic projections that lack realistic assumptions and scenario analysis

faq.question: What information do I need to provide before generating a business case?
faq.answer: At minimum: company name and description, stage (pre-seed, seed, Series A), problem being solved, target customers, and funding goal. For best results, also provide existing pitch deck, market research, financial model, and competitive analysis if available.

faq.question: How long does it take to generate a complete business case?
faq.answer: A comprehensive business case typically takes 1-2 hours to create with all 10 sections. The process includes information gathering, analysis, document creation, and review cycles for refinement.

faq.question: Can this skill verify market data and competitor information?
faq.answer: No. This skill structures and formats information you provide. You are responsible for verifying market data accuracy, competitor analysis, and financial assumptions. Always cite sources and validate external data independently.

faq.question: What format will the business case be delivered in?
faq.answer: Business cases are generated as markdown documents that can be easily converted to PDF or Word format. The output follows a structured template with clear sections, tables for financial data, and formatting suitable for investor distribution.

faq.question: How often should I update my business case document?
faq.answer: Update your business case quarterly to reflect current metrics and milestones. Also refresh before each funding round, major pivot, or significant business change. Keep the executive summary and traction sections most current.

faq.question: Can this skill help with pitch deck creation too?
faq.answer: Yes. The business case content can be adapted into pitch deck slides. The skill can provide an executive summary slide and suggest how to condense each section into presentation format with key visuals and talking points.
